description: Spent multiple rounds debugging Plaud transcription trigger failure ('start trans task error', status=-1/-12) by inspecting API parameters, trying is_reload variants, examining ori_ready fields, and writing debug scripts — when the actual cause was simply that David is out of Plaud transcription minutes. Jarvis never considered the simplest explanation (account quota exhausted) and instead chased increasingly technical API theories.
correction: When a transcription trigger fails with a generic error, check account-level issues first: subscription status, remaining minutes/credits, plan limits. The simplest explanation is usually correct.
systemic_fix: Add to Plaud troubleshooting: if /ai/transsumm returns status=-1 or status=-12 with 'start trans task error', the FIRST thing to check is whether the account has remaining transcription minutes. Add a quota check step to the fetch script before attempting triggers. Also: ask David before spending 10 minutes debugging an API.
